rationale: The citing paper is framed as follow-up research to earlier feature-fatigue findings, explicitly restating the prior result that consumers prefer feature-rich options before use but shift away after use because of usability costs. It then adds a new explanation and boundary conditions (social utility, public display vs public performance) on top of that baseline effect. This indicates the cited work is used to motivate and anchor the new contribution, not merely listed as general background.
relation_description: The citing paper builds directly on the cited paper's feature-fatigue effect and extends it by introducing social-impression motives as a driver of feature-rich choices. It further identifies when this preference reverses, namely when consumers expect to use the product publicly and usability concerns dominate.
